Inspect attack paths from Terraform/OpenTofu plans and optional cloud
context. This command is review-oriented: it renders deterministic path evidence
without evaluating deployment policy or returning a block exit code.

Usage:
  changegate attack-paths --plan tfplan.json [flags]
  changegate attack-paths [command]

Available Commands:
  visualize   Write a self-contained interactive HTML attack-path visualization

Flags:
      --cloud-context string   optional cloud context provider: aws
      --context-file string    offline cloud context snapshot file
  -h, --help                   help for attack-paths
      --max-depth int          maximum graph path depth (default 12)
      --max-paths int          maximum attack paths to return (default 25)
      --plan stringArray       path to Terraform/OpenTofu plan JSON produced by show -json; repeat for multiple plans
      --principal string       only include attack paths starting from this IAM principal
      --to-sensitive-data      only include public-to-sensitive-data attack paths

Global Flags:
      --cache-dir string   cache directory for policy packs and cloud context
      --debug              enable debug diagnostics without secrets
      --format string      output format: table, json, sarif, junit, markdown, github-step-summary, github-annotations, gitlab-code-quality, pr-comment, audit-bundle, dot, mermaid (default "table")
      --mode string        enforcement mode: block, warn, audit (default "block")
      --no-color           disable ANSI color output
      --out string         write output to a file
      --policy string      path to .changegate.yaml policy file
      --quiet              suppress non-essential human output
      --verbose            enable informational diagnostics

Use "changegate attack-paths [command] --help" for more information about a command.
